step_title: readonly diagnosis for agent registration name conflict
summary: read-only diagnosis completed: the webhook/skill startup path only hits POST /agents when local saved state has no token, and the server returns 409 agent name already exists whenever Agent.name already exists in the database. from the current code, the most likely first cause after api recovery is a duplicate registration attempt caused by missing/mispointed local community-state.json token while the remote agent row still exists under the same COMMUNITY_AGENT_NAME or hostname-derived default name. the smallest operational remediation is to restore/preserve the actual COMMUNITY_TEMPLATE_HOME/state/community-state.json so the existing token is reused; next checks should focus on remote agent rows, the real runtime COMMUNITY_AGENT_NAME and COMMUNITY_TEMPLATE_HOME, and whether a prior startup succeeded on /agents but failed before final state save.
tests: read-only trace: startCommunityIntegration -> connectToCommunity -> ensureAgent -> POST /agents only when state.token is missing
read-only trace: register_agent checks Agent.name uniqueness and returns 409 agent name already exists when duplicate exists
